title: Suppressed Noise: Trusted Scanner Traffic
learning_objectives: Understand why some noisy activity should stay visible but not alert
Practice checking allowlist logic and suppression conditions
Distinguish between suspicious-looking requests and actual case-worthy alerts
triage_questions: Why do these requests look suspicious at first glance?
What field keeps them from becoming an alert in this lab?
What is the analyst takeaway when something looks noisy but is expected?
evidence_to_check: The requested paths and failed responses
The user agent value on the web events
The allowlist label and the absence of a matching alert
hints: The paths are meant to look noisy.
Pay attention to the user agent and the allowlist-related fields.
This case is about suppression logic working as designed.
escalation_guidance: Do not escalate if the allowlisted scanner context holds. Document it as expected noisy traffic that remained suppressed.
